justification: All pass criteria met: prototype pollution identified by name, __proto__ attack vector explained with specific payload ({"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}}), global Object.prototype impact explained (pollutes every object in the process for its lifetime), safe merge fix recommended (Object.hasOwn guard + reject __proto__/constructor/prototype keys, plus schema validation), safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Exact attack payload provided with clear reproduction steps
Explains that for..in iterates __proto__ and recursion pollutes Object.prototype globally
Fix is specific and correct: Object.hasOwn check plus key blocklist, with schema validation as defense-in-depth
Correctly identifies additional findings (falsy-value merge bug, mass assignment) beyond the primary vulnerability
safe_to_deploy is false with clear rationale about immediate exploitability
Suggests replacing hand-rolled deepMerge with flat Object.assign since preferences are single-level — practical and correct
